title: Financial Mathematics
blurb: Compound growth and decay, nominal and effective rates, annuities, loans, outstanding balances and sinking-fund style timelines.

questions.context: A delivery bakkie is bought for R380 000. Its value depreciates at $14\%$ per annum on the reducing-balance method. The price of a new bakkie of the same model increases with inflation at $6\%$ per annum.

parts.q: Calculate the value of the bakkie after $5$ years. Round to the nearest cent.
Calculate the price of a NEW bakkie of the same model after $5$ years. Round to the nearest cent.

questions.context: Bank A offers an interest rate of $10{,}5\%$ per annum, compounded monthly. Bank B offers $10{,}8\%$ per annum, compounded annually.

parts.q: Convert Bank A's rate to an effective annual interest rate. Give your answer correct to TWO decimal places.
Which bank offers the better rate for an investor? Justify your answer.
solution: Bank A's effective rate is $11{,}02\%$, Bank B's effective rate is $10{,}8\%$
$11{,}02\%>10{,}8\%$, so Bank A is better for an investor
parts.answer: Bank A, since its effective rate ($11{,}02\%$) exceeds Bank B's $10{,}8\%$

questions.context: Thandi wants to save for a deposit on a flat. She pays R1 500 into a savings account at the END of every month for $6$ years. The account earns interest at $9\%$ per annum, compounded monthly.

parts.q: Calculate the value of Thandi's savings at the end of the $6$ years. Round to the nearest cent.
How much interest does Thandi earn over the $6$ years?

questions.context: The Dlamini family takes out a home loan of R950 000 to buy a house in Soweto. Interest is charged at $11\%$ per annum, compounded monthly, and the loan is repaid with equal monthly payments over $20$ years, starting one month after the loan is granted.

parts.q: Calculate the monthly payment. Round to the nearest cent.
Calculate the outstanding balance of the loan immediately after the $96$th payment (i.e. after $8$ years). Round to the nearest cent.
solution: $=$ R$782\ 238{,}29$ (nearest cent, using the rounded payment of R$9\ 805{,}79$; using the unrounded payment gives R$782\ 238{,}33$)
parts.answer: R782 238,29 (accept small differences from rounding the monthly payment)

parts.q: Calculate the TOTAL interest paid over the full $20$ years if all $240$ payments are made as scheduled.

questions.context: Sipho opens a savings account with a single deposit of R20 000. From the end of the first month he also deposits R500 at the END of every month for $5$ years. The account earns $8\%$ per annum, compounded monthly. Exactly $3$ years after opening the account he withdraws R10 000 to repair his taxi.

parts.q: Calculate the value that the initial R20 000 deposit alone grows to at the end of the $5$ years. Round to the nearest cent.
Calculate the balance in the account at the end of the $5$ years, taking ALL the transactions into account. Round to the nearest cent.
solution: The R$10\ 000$ withdrawal accumulates interest against the account for the remaining $2$ years ($24$ months): $10\ 000\left(1+\dfrac{0{,}08}{12}\right)^{24}=$ R$11\ 728{,}88$

questions.context: Lerato invests R25 000 in a fixed-deposit account that pays interest at $7{,}5\%$ per annum, compounded quarterly.

parts.q: Calculate the value of the investment after $6$ years. Round to the nearest cent.
How long, in years (correct to TWO decimal places), will it take an investment to DOUBLE in value at $8\%$ per annum, compounded annually?
Convert a nominal interest rate of $9{,}6\%$ per annum, compounded quarterly, to an effective annual interest rate. Give your answer correct to TWO decimal places.
Determine the nominal interest rate, compounded monthly, that is equivalent to an effective annual rate of $12\%$. Give your answer correct to TWO decimal places.
solution: Nominal rate $\approx 11{,}39\%$ per annum, compounded monthly

questions.context: Zanele buys a car with a loan of R285 000. Interest is charged at $12{,}5\%$ per annum, compounded monthly. She repays the loan with equal monthly instalments over $6$ years, starting one month after the loan is granted.

parts.q: Calculate Zanele's monthly instalment. Round to the nearest cent.
Calculate the TOTAL amount of interest Zanele pays over the $6$ years.

questions.context: Bongani inherits R750 000 and invests it in an account earning $9\%$ per annum, compounded monthly. Starting one month after the investment is made, he withdraws R8 000 at the end of EVERY month.

parts.q: How many withdrawals of the FULL R8 000 will Bongani be able to make?
solution: $i=\dfrac{0{,}09}{12}=0{,}0075$; the investment is the present value of the withdrawals: $750\ 000=\dfrac{8\ 000\left[1-(1{,}0075)^{-n}\right]}{0{,}0075}$
He can make $162$ withdrawals of the full R$8\ 000$

parts.q: Calculate the balance in the account immediately after the last full withdrawal. Round to the nearest cent.
solution: (One month later this grows to only R$4\ 266{,}21$, which is less than R$8\ 000$ — confirming that a $163$rd full withdrawal is impossible)

questions.context: The Mokoena family takes out a home loan of R600 000 at $10{,}5\%$ per annum, compounded monthly. The loan is to be repaid with equal monthly instalments over $20$ years, starting one month after the loan is granted.

parts.q: Calculate the monthly instalment. Round to the nearest cent.

Immediately after the $60$th instalment, the family pays an EXTRA lump sum of R100 000 into the loan account. The monthly instalment remains unchanged. Calculate how many months EARLIER the loan will be settled.
solution: After the lump sum the balance is $541\ 911{,}03-100\ 000=$ R$441\ 911{,}03$
Let $n$ be the number of remaining payments: $441\ 911{,}03=\dfrac{5\ 990{,}28\left[1-(1{,}00875)^{-n}\right]}{0{,}00875}$
So $120$ more payments are needed ($119$ full instalments plus one final smaller payment), instead of the $240-60=180$ that remained
The loan is settled $180-120=60$ months ($5$ years) earlier

questions.context: A printing company in Durban buys a machine for R450 000. The machine depreciates at $15\%$ per annum on the reducing-balance method and must be replaced after $8$ years. The price of a new machine increases at $6\%$ per annum. The old machine will be sold at its scrap value after $8$ years, and the company sets up a sinking fund to cover the shortfall. The fund earns $8{,}25\%$ per annum, compounded monthly. Equal monthly payments are made into the fund, starting one month after the machine is bought and ending at the end of the $8$ years.

parts.q: Calculate the scrap value of the machine after $8$ years. Round to the nearest cent.
Calculate the price of a NEW machine after $8$ years. Round to the nearest cent.
Calculate the monthly payment into the sinking fund. Round to the nearest cent.
